misc.moonAdvNvl: 🌱 Sowing & transplanting favoured. Vegetative rest — avoid pruning. Root activity at maximum.
misc.moonAdvCroissNaiss: 🌿 Ascending phase. Sap rises — ideal for grafting and cuttings. Avoid pruning.
misc.moonAdvPremCroiss: 💧 Effective watering — optimal absorption. Good for foliar fertilisation.
misc.moonAdvPremQ: ✂️ Neutral moment. Light pruning acceptable. Full aerial activity.
misc.moonAdvGibbCroiss: 🌸 Maximum energy in the leaves. NPK fertilisation very effective.
misc.moonAdvPleine: ⚡ Energy peak. Optimal P & K fertilisation. Do not prune. Quality harvest.
misc.moonAdvGibbDec: 🪴 Sap descending — pruning possible, rapid healing. Good for repotting.
misc.moonAdvDernQ: ✂️ Pruning recommended — moderate regrowth, less stress. Effective phyto treatment.
misc.moonAdvDernCroiss: 🌱 Root phase. Reduced watering. Deep soil amendments (compost, lime) very effective.
misc.moonAdvFinCycle: 🌱 Vegetative rest. Ideal for wintering and planting. Root activity at maximum.

desc.7: Fruit set to 90% final size (sigmoidal curve)
desc.9: Growth end, leaf fall, winter rest

sub.10: First leaves separate, scales open, leaves emerge
sub.15: More leaves visible, final size not reached
sub.51: Inflorescence buds swollen, closed, light green scales
sub.53: Buds burst, first flower buds visible
sub.55: Flowers closed (green bud), solitary or in racemes
sub.56: Petals elongating, sepals cover corolla halfway (white bud)
sub.57: Sepals spreading, white or pink petals increasingly visible
sub.59: Flowers form a hollow ball with petals
sub.65: Full bloom: ~50% flowers open, first petals falling
sub.69: End of flowering: all petals fallen
sub.71: Fruit set: ovary swelling, beginning of abscission
sub.72: Small green fruit crowned by sepals
sub.74: Dark green fruit, 40% final size. End of physiological drop
sub.83: Fruit ready to harvest, varietal color not reached
sub.89: Consumption maturity, characteristic texture and taste
sub.91: End of shoot growth, foliage entirely green
sub.93: Beginning of senescence, old leaf drop
sub.00: Dormancy: undifferentiated buds, closed, covered with green scales
sub.03: End of swelling: scales slightly separated

fert.7: Resume with increased K and Ca

reg.0: Sensitive to gibberellins and cytokinins during dormancy
reg.1: Leaf development promoted by gibberellic acid
reg.6: Gibberellins recommended to improve fruit set (sensitive varieties)
reg.7: Auxins to improve fruit development (2nd period)
reg.8: Ethylene: coloring. Gibberellins + auxins: delay senescence

rem.0: 3-4 flushes per year in climates with marked winters. Continuous in warm climates. Only the 1st spring flush induces flowering (except citron and lime: flowering possible on 2nd summer generation)
rem.1: Stages 11-15 very sensitive to aphids. In citrus, "visible" replaces "spread" as leaves spread early
rem.3: 3-4 shoots in subtropical, more in tropical. Leaves at final size → foliar applications recommended spring/summer. Secondary shoots then successive series
rem.5: Duration depends on thermal conditions. For some varieties, stages 5 and 6 coexist: flowers at different stages on the same tree
rem.6: Anthesis ~7 days. Not simultaneous: depends on inflorescence type and position on the tree
rem.7: Sigmoidal curve with 3 periods (Bain, 1958). 1st: cell division. 2nd: cell expansion. Physiological drop at stage 73. Duration: 4-7 months
rem.8: 3rd sigmoidal period. Color and taste not simultaneous. Harvest possible from stage 83. Senescence delayable by gibberellins + auxins
rem.9: Persistent leaves, lifespan 17-24 months depending on variety and climate

drip.title: Irrigation systems
drip.name: Circuit name
drip.separateHint: Separate plants with very different needs

sync.manualBackupIntro: This feature is now optional. Your data is automatically saved to the CitrusCodex server. This encrypted option is still available for personal backup independent of the server (encrypted export to GitHub Gist or WebDAV).
sync.migrationIntro: Your local data ({n} subjects) will be saved to the server so it is accessible from all your devices.
sync.migrationConflictIntro: You have {local} subjects locally and {server} subjects on the server. What would you like to do?

wikiV1.backupIntro: Your locally stored personal wiki has been archived. Shared contributions are in Community → Citrus Wiki. This archive will be deleted in {days} day(s) — download it if you want to keep your notes.
